req.title: Allergy intolerance
req.statement: A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient; subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-009 requires: (1) a clinician can POST a valid AllergyIntolerance linked to a patient and receive 201 with the resource stored; (2) a subsequent MedicationRequest for a conflicting substance triggers a clinical alert; (3) a duplicate POST returns 409. Four tests are designed: EP-positive for the happy path store+alert flow, NEG for duplicate detection, BVA for the minimal-valid payload (single required field boundary), and STC to verify the state transition from 'allergy recorded' to 'alert active' when a conflicting MedicationRequest is created. Each test is idempotent via unique patient/allergy IDs generated per run.

tests.name: test_post_allergy_intolerance_valid_returns_201_and_stores_resource
preconditions: Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_token fixture>
A unique patient ID is pre-created via <create_patient fixture>
No existing AllergyIntolerance for this patient/substance pair
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with a well-formed FHIR AllergyIntolerance payload referencing the patient and substance code 'penicillin'
expected: GET returns HTTP 200 with matching patient reference and substance code
tests.executable:
import pytest, uuid
from <helper_fn> import api_client, create_patient, clinician_token

def test_post_allergy_intolerance_valid_returns_201_and_stores_resource():
    client = api_client(token=clinician_token())
    patient_id = create_patient(client)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "7980", "display": "Penicillin"}]},
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "confirmed"}]}
    }
    resp = client.post("/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "AllergyIntolerance"
    allergy_id = body["id"]
    get_resp = client.get(f"/AllergyIntolerance/{allergy_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    stored = get_resp.json()
    assert stored["patient"]["reference"] == f"Patient/{patient_id}"
    assert stored["code"]["coding"][0]["code"] == "7980"

tests.name: test_post_duplicate_allergy_intolerance_returns_409
preconditions: Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_token fixture>
A unique patient ID is pre-created via <create_patient fixture>
An AllergyIntolerance for the same patient/substance has already been posted successfully
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with identical patient reference and substance code as the pre-existing record
Inspect the HTTP status code and error body
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error indicating duplicate conflict
trace: R-HLT-009: error pathway — 409 when exact duplicate
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, create_patient, clinician_token

def test_post_duplicate_allergy_intolerance_returns_409():
    client = api_client(token=clinician_token())
    patient_id = create_patient(client)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "7980", "display": "Penicillin"}]},
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "confirmed"}]}
    }
    first = client.post("/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload)
    assert first.status_code == 201, f"Setup failed: {first.status_code}"
    second = client.post("/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload)
    assert second.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409 on duplicate, got {second.status_code}"
    body = second.json()
    assert "resourceType" in body or "error" in body

tests.name: test_post_allergy_intolerance_minimal_payload_boundary
preconditions: Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_token fixture>
A unique patient ID is pre-created via <create_patient fixture>
Payload contains only the minimum required FHIR fields (patient + code); all optional fields omitted
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with only 'resourceType', 'patient', and 'code' fields populated
Verify acceptance at the lower field-count boundary
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 (minimal valid resource is accepted)
trace: BVA: minimum-field boundary of valid AllergyIntolerance

tests.name: test_medication_request_triggers_conflict_alert_after_allergy_recorded
preconditions: Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_token fixture>
A unique patient ID is pre-created via <create_patient fixture>
No prior AllergyIntolerance or MedicationRequest for this patient
actions: Assert 201 to confirm state transition to 'allergy active'
POST /MedicationRequest for same patient with medication 'Amoxicillin' (RxNorm 723)
Inspect response for conflict alert indicator
expected: MedicationRequest response body or headers contain a conflict alert (detectedIssue, warning header, or alerts array)
trace: R-HLT-009: subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict
STC: state transition from 'no allergy' -> 'allergy recorded' -> 'conflict alert active'
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, create_patient, clinician_token

def test_medication_request_triggers_conflict_alert_after_allergy_recorded():
    client = api_client(token=clinician_token())
    patient_id = create_patient(client)
    allergy_payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "723", "display": "Amoxicillin"}]},
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "confirmed"}]}
    }
    allergy_resp = client.post("/AllergyIntolerance", json=allergy_payload)
    assert allergy_resp.status_code == 201
    med_payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm", "code": "723", "display": "Amoxicillin"}]}
    }
    med_resp = client.post("/MedicationRequest", json=med_payload)
    assert med_resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {med_resp.status_code}"
    body = med_resp.json()
    has_alert = (
        "detectedIssue" in body
        or "alerts" in body
        or "X-Clinical-Warning" in med_resp.headers
        or any("conflict" in str(v).lower() for v in body.values())
    )
    assert has_alert, f"No conflict alert found in response: {body}"
